solution:
Let the potential be x V at the junction.

By using V=IR we get,

30-x=10i1x-12=20i2x-2=30i3

By using Kirchhoff's law,

i1=i2+i3

⇒30-x10+12-x20+2-x30=0⇒90-3x+18-1.5x+2-x30=0

⇒x=20 V

The current through the 20 Ω resistor is 

i2=30-1220 A

⇒i2=820A=0.4 A

So, x=1

subject: Physics
solution:
In steady state, the capacitor acts as an open circuit, meaning no current flows through the middle branch.

The circuit simplifies to a single loop containing the $2 \text{ V}$ battery, the $6 \Omega$ resistor, and the $2 \Omega$ resistor.

The total resistance of this loop is $R_{\text{eq}} = 2 + 6 = 8 \Omega$.

The current flowing through the loop is $I = \dfrac{2}{8} = 0.25 \text{ A}$.

The potential difference across the parallel branches is equal to the voltage drop across the $2 \Omega$ resistor:
$V = I \times 2 = 0.25 \times 2 = 0.5 \text{ V}$

Since there is no current in the middle branch, the voltage drop across the $4 \Omega$ resistor is zero. Thus, the entire potential difference of $0.5 \text{ V}$ appears across the capacitor.

Answer: $0.5$

subject: Physics
solution:
Let resistance S is connected with galvanometer of resistance G, than

IG=VR+G


Now, as shown below figure a shunt S is connected in parallel with the galvanometer for half deflection in it, that is IG' = IG2, than

 I=VR+GSG+S


As, shunt and galvanometer are in parallel  then VG=VS
IG'G=I-IG'S

⇒IG'G+S=IS
⇒IG2=ISG+S     ∵IG'=IG2

On, substituting the values of IG and I in above equation

V2R+G=VR+GSG+S×SG+S
⇒12R+G=SRG+S+GS
⇒RG+S+GS=2SR+G
⇒RG+RS+GS=2SR+G
⇒RG=2SR+G-SR+G
⇒RG=SR+G

subject: Physics
solution:
We know $\mathrm{R}=\frac{\rho l}{\mathrm{~A}}, \mathrm{R} \propto \frac{l}{\mathrm{r}^2}$
As we starch the wire, its length will increase but its radius will decrease keeping the volume constant
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{i}}=\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{f}} \\
& \pi \mathrm{r}^2 l=\pi \frac{\mathrm{r}^2}{4} l_{\mathrm{f}} \\
& l_{\mathrm{f}}=4 l \\
& \frac{\mathrm{R}_{\text {new }}}{\mathrm{R}_{\text {old }}}=\left(\frac{4 l}{\frac{\mathrm{r}^2}{4}}\right) \frac{\mathrm{r}^2}{l}=16 \\
& \mathrm{R}_{\text {new }}=16 \mathrm{R} \\
\therefore & \mathrm{x}=16
\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
The resistance in each branch is 6 Ω. They are in parallel. So, net resistance is R'=62=3 Ω.

The total current flowing through the loop,

I=VR'=9 V3 Ω=3 A

Thus, through each branch the current is 1.5 A.

Hence, the voltage difference across the points is

VA-VB=4×1.5-2×1.5=3 V

solution:
As we know, voltmeter is connected in parallel to the element being measured.

The equivalent resistance of the above circuit is Req=400×100400+100+100=80+100=180 Ω

Current through the circuit is i=90180=12 A

Reading of the voltmeter is VR=i400×100400+100=12×80=40 V
